other_observers: Tony Leukering, Mark Peterson, Lisa Edwards; No, I was not the original finder of this bird.
description: It was a dark bird, sitting on the water.  Its proportions looked similar to those of a Sabine's Gull, with a petite head and bill.  It had a smooth pattern to its neck (both front and back) and breast, with no streaking.  Its flanks had clean, bold black and white barring.  By direct comparison, it appeared to be 5% smaller than an adjacent Franklin's Gull, while both were sitting on the water.Behaviors: Sitting on the water.Call: none heardPlumage: immature
